tEn: Eureka: Human-Level Reward Design via Coding Large Language Models
tZh: Eureka：通过编码大型语言模型实现人类级别的奖励设计
sEn: Eureka uses LLMs to automatically generate reward functions that outperform human-designed ones, enabling complex manipulation skills like pen spinning.
sZh: Eureka利用大型语言模型自动生成奖励函数，其性能超越人类专家设计的奖励，并首次实现模拟Shadow Hand进行快速笔旋转技巧。
absEn: Large Language Models (LLMs) have excelled as high-level semantic planners for sequential decision-making tasks. However, harnessing them to learn complex low-level manipulation tasks, such as dexterous pen spinning, remains an open problem. We bridge this fundamental gap and present Eureka, a human-level reward design algorithm powered by LLMs. Eureka exploits the remarkable zero-shot generation, code-writing, and in-context improvement capabilities of state-of-the-art LLMs, such as GPT-4, to perform evolutionary optimization over reward code. The resulting rewards can then be used to acquire complex skills via reinforcement learning. Without any task-specific prompting or pre-defined reward templates, Eureka generates reward functions that outperform expert human-engineered rewards. In a diverse suite of 29 open-source RL environments that include 10 distinct robot morphologies, Eureka outperforms human experts on 83% of the tasks, leading to an average normalized improvement of 52%. The generality of Eureka also enables a new gradient-free in-context learning approach to reinforcement learning from human feedback (RLHF), readily incorporating human inputs to improve the quality and the safety of the generated rewards without model updating. Finally, using Eureka rewards in a curriculum learning setting, we demonstrate for the first time, a simulated Shadow Hand capable of performing pen spinning tricks, adeptly manipulating a pen in circles at rapid speed.
absZh: 大型语言模型（LLM）在顺序决策任务中作为高级语义规划器表现出色。然而，如何利用它们学习复杂的低级操作任务（如灵巧的笔旋转）仍然是一个开放问题。我们弥合了这一基本差距，提出了Eureka——一种由LLM驱动的人类级别奖励设计算法。Eureka利用最先进的LLM（如GPT-4）卓越的零样本生成、代码编写和上下文改进能力，对奖励代码进行进化优化。生成的奖励随后可通过强化学习获取复杂技能。无需任何特定任务提示或预定义奖励模板，Eureka生成的奖励函数优于专家手工设计的奖励。在包含10种不同机器人形态的29个开源RL环境套件中，Eureka在83%的任务上优于人类专家，平均标准化改进达52%。Eureka的通用性还实现了一种新的无梯度上下文学习方法，用于从人类反馈中进行强化学习（RLHF），无需模型更新即可轻松融入人类输入以提高生成奖励的质量和安全性。最后，在课程学习设置中使用Eureka奖励，我们首次展示了模拟Shadow Hand能够执行笔旋转技巧，以快速速度熟练地操纵笔进行圆周运动。

contrib.en: Proposes Eureka, a human-level reward design algorithm using LLMs for evolutionary optimization over reward code.
contrib.zh: 提出Eureka，一种利用LLM对奖励代码进行进化优化的人类级奖励设计算法。

contrib.en: Outperforms human experts on 83% of 29 RL tasks with 52% average normalized improvement.
contrib.zh: 在29个RL任务中的83%上超越人类专家，平均归一化提升52%。

contrib.en: Enables gradient-free in-context RLHF, incorporating human feedback without model updating.
contrib.zh: 实现无梯度的上下文RLHF，无需模型更新即可融入人类反馈。

contrib.en: Demonstrates first simulated Shadow Hand pen spinning via curriculum learning with Eureka rewards.
contrib.zh: 首次通过课程学习结合Eureka奖励实现模拟Shadow Hand转笔。

contrib.en: Introduces reward reflection for targeted reward improvement via textual policy training summaries.
contrib.zh: 引入奖励反思，通过文本策略训练摘要实现针对性奖励改进。

contrib.en: Achieves zero-shot reward generation from environment source code without task-specific prompts.
contrib.zh: 从环境源代码实现零样本奖励生成，无需任务特定提示。

limits.en: Requires environment source code as input, limiting applicability to closed-source environments.
limits.zh: 需要环境源代码作为输入，限制了在闭源环境中的适用性。

limits.en: Evolutionary search relies on GPU-accelerated RL, which may be computationally expensive.
limits.zh: 进化搜索依赖GPU加速的RL，计算成本可能较高。

limits.en: Performance depends on the quality of the backbone LLM (e.g., GPT-4), which may not be accessible.
limits.zh: 性能依赖于骨干LLM（如GPT-4）的质量，可能不易获取。

limits.en: Reward reflection requires tracking individual reward components, adding implementation complexity.
limits.zh: 奖励反思需要跟踪单个奖励组件，增加了实现复杂性。

limits.en: May not generalize to tasks with non-code-based fitness functions or sparse feedback.
limits.zh: 可能无法泛化到非代码适应度函数或稀疏反馈的任务。

items.en: Large Language Models (LLMs) have excelled as high-level semantic planners for sequential decision-making tasks. However, harnessing them to learn complex low-level manipulation tasks, such as dexterous pen spinning, remains an open problem. We bridge this fundamental gap and present \ourmethod, a human-level reward design algorithm powered by LLMs. \ourmethodexploits the remarkable zero-shot generation, code-writing, and in-context improvement capabilities of state-of-the-art LLMs, such as GPT-4, to perform evolutionary optimization over reward code. The resulting rewards can then be used to acquire complex skills via reinforcement learning. Without any task-specific prompting or pre-defined reward templates, \ourmethodgenerates reward functions that outperform expert human-engineered rewards. In a diverse suite of 29 open-source RL environments that include 10 distinct robot morphologies, \ourmethodoutperforms human experts on 83% of the tasks, leading to an average normalized improvement of 52%. The generality of \ourmethodalso enables a new gradient-free in-context learning approach to reinforcement learning from human feedback (RLHF), readily incorporating human inputs to improve the quality and the safety of the generated rewards without model updating. Finally, using \ourmethodrewards in a curriculum learning setting, we demonstrate for the first time, a simulated Shadow Hand capable of performing pen spinning tricks, adeptly manipulating a pen in circles at rapid speed.
items.zh: 大型语言模型（LLM）在顺序决策任务中作为高级语义规划器表现出色。然而，如何利用它们学习复杂的低级操作任务（例如灵巧的笔旋转）仍然是一个未解决的问题。我们弥合了这一基本差距，并提出了我们的方法，一种由LLM驱动的人类级奖励设计算法。我们的方法利用了最先进的LLM（如GPT-4）卓越的零样本生成、代码编写和上下文内改进能力，对奖励代码进行进化优化。由此产生的奖励可以通过强化学习用于获取复杂技能。无需任何特定任务的提示或预定义的奖励模板，我们的方法生成的奖励函数优于专家手工设计的奖励。在包含10种不同机器人形态的29个开源RL环境套件中，我们的方法在83%的任务上优于人类专家，平均归一化改进率为52%。我们的方法的通用性还实现了一种新的无梯度上下文内学习方法，用于基于人类反馈的强化学习（RLHF），无需模型更新即可轻松融入人类输入，以提高生成奖励的质量和安全性。最后，在课程学习设置中使用我们的方法奖励，我们首次展示了模拟Shadow Hand能够执行笔旋转技巧，熟练地快速旋转笔。

items.capEn: Figure 1: \ourmethodgenerates human-level reward functions across diverse robots and tasks. Combined with curriculum learning, \ourmethodfor the first time, unlocks rapid pen-spinning capabilities on an anthropomorphic five-finger hand. Figures rendered using Omniverse (NVIDIA, 2023).
items.capZh: 图1：我们的方法在多种机器人和任务中生成人类级的奖励函数。结合课程学习，我们的方法首次在拟人五指手上实现了快速笔旋转能力。使用Omniverse（NVIDIA，2023）渲染的图形。

items.en: Large Language Models (LLMs) have excelled as high-level semantic planners for robotics tasks (Ahn et al., 2022; Singh et al., 2023), but whether they can be used to learn complex low-level manipulation tasks, such as dexterous pen spinning, remains an open problem. Existing attempts require substantial domain expertise to construct task prompts or learn only simple skills (Yu et al., 2023; Brohan et al., 2023), leaving a substantial gap in achieving human-level dexterity.
items.zh: 大型语言模型（LLM）在机器人任务中作为高级语义规划器表现出色（Ahn等人，2022；Singh等人，2023），但它们能否用于学习复杂的低级操作任务（例如灵巧的笔旋转）仍然是一个未解决的问题。现有的尝试需要大量的领域专业知识来构建任务提示，或者只能学习简单的技能（Yu等人，2023；Brohan等人，2023），在实现人类级灵巧性方面存在巨大差距。

items.en: On the other hand, reinforcement learning (RL) has achieved impressive results in dexterity (Andrychowicz et al., 2020; Handa et al., 2023) as well as many other domains—if the human designers can carefully construct reward functions that accurately codify and provide learning signals for the desired behavior. As many real-world RL tasks admit sparse rewards that are difficult for learning, reward shaping that provides incremental learning signals is necessary in practice (Ng et al., 1999). Despite their fundamental importance, reward functions are known to be notoriously difficult to design (Russell & Norvig, 1995; Sutton & Barto, 2018); a recent survey conducted finds 92% of polled reinforcement learning researchers and practitioners report manual trial-and-error reward design and 89% indicate that their designed rewards are sub-optimal (Booth et al., 2023) and lead to unintended behavior (Hadfield-Menell et al., 2017).
items.zh: 另一方面，强化学习（RL）在灵巧性（Andrychowicz等人，2020；Handa等人，2023）以及许多其他领域取得了令人印象深刻的结果——前提是人类设计者能够精心构建奖励函数，准确编码并为期望行为提供学习信号。由于许多现实世界的RL任务具有稀疏奖励，难以学习，因此提供增量学习信号的奖励塑形在实践中是必要的（Ng等人，1999）。尽管奖励函数至关重要，但众所周知，它们的设计极其困难（Russell & Norvig，1995；Sutton & Barto，2018）；最近的一项调查发现，92%的受访强化学习研究人员和从业者报告手动试错奖励设计，89%表示他们设计的奖励是次优的（Booth等人，2023），并导致意外行为（Hadfield-Menell等人，2017）。

items.en: Given the paramount importance of reward design, we ask whether it is possible to develop a universal reward programming algorithm using state-of-the-art coding LLMs, such as GPT-4 (OpenAI, 2023). Their remarkable abilities in code writing, zero-shot generation, and in-context learning have previously enabled effective programmatic agents (Shinn et al., 2023; Wang et al., 2023a). Ideally, this reward design algorithm should achieve human-level reward generation capabilities that scale to a broad spectrum of tasks, automate the tedious trial-and-error procedure without human supervision, and yet be compatible with human oversight to assure safety and alignment.
items.zh: 鉴于奖励设计的极端重要性，我们询问是否可能使用最先进的编码LLM（如GPT-4）开发一种通用的奖励编程算法（OpenAI，2023）。它们在代码编写、零样本生成和上下文内学习方面的卓越能力此前已实现了有效的程序化智能体（Shinn等人，2023；Wang等人，2023a）。理想情况下，这种奖励设计算法应实现人类级的奖励生成能力，可扩展到广泛的任务，自动化繁琐的试错过程而无需人工监督，同时兼容人类监督以确保安全性和对齐。

items.capEn: Figure 2: \ourmethodtakes unmodified environment source code and language task description as context to zero-shot generate executable reward functions from a coding LLM. Then, it iterates between reward sampling, GPU-accelerated reward evaluation, and reward reflection to progressively improve its reward outputs.
items.capZh: 图2：我们的方法将未经修改的环境源代码和语言任务描述作为上下文，从编码LLM零样本生成可执行的奖励函数。然后，它在奖励采样、GPU加速奖励评估和奖励反思之间迭代，以逐步改进其奖励输出。

items.en: We introduce Evolution-driven Universal REward Kit for Agent (\ourmethod), a novel reward design algorithm powered by coding LLMs with the following contributions:
items.zh: 我们引入了进化驱动的通用奖励工具包（我们的方法），一种由编码LLM驱动的新型奖励设计算法，具有以下贡献：

items.en: Achieves human-level performance on reward design across a diverse suite of 29 open-sourced RL environments that include 10 distinct robot morphologies, including quadruped, quadcopter, biped, manipulator, as well as several dexterous hands; see Fig. 1. Without any task-specific prompting or reward templates, \ourmethodautonomously generates rewards that outperform expert human rewards on 83% of the tasks and realizes an average normalized improvement of 52%.
items.zh: 在包含10种不同机器人形态（包括四足、四旋翼、双足、机械手以及多种灵巧手）的29个开源RL环境套件中，实现了人类级的奖励设计性能；参见图1。无需任何特定任务的提示或奖励模板，我们的方法自主生成奖励，在83%的任务上优于专家手工奖励，平均归一化改进率为52%。

items.en: Solves dexterous manipulation tasks that were previously not feasible by manual reward engineering. We consider pen spinning, in which a five-finger hand needs to rapidly rotate a pen in pre-defined spinning configurations for as many cycles as possible. Combining \ourmethodwith curriculum learning, we demonstrate for the first time rapid pen spinning maneuvers on a simulated anthropomorphic Shadow Hand (see Figure 1 bottom).
items.zh: 解决了以前通过手动奖励工程无法实现的灵巧操作任务。我们考虑笔旋转，其中五指手需要在预定义的旋转配置中快速旋转笔，尽可能多地旋转圈数。结合我们的方法与课程学习，我们首次在模拟拟人Shadow Hand上展示了快速笔旋转操作（见图1底部）。

items.en: Enables a new gradient-free in-context learning approach to reinforcement learning from human feedback (RLHF) that can generate more performant and human-aligned reward functions based on various forms of human inputs. We demonstrate that \ourmethodcan readily benefit from and improve upon existing human reward functions. Likewise, we showcase \ourmethod’s ability to use human textual feedback to co-pilot reward function designs that capture the nuanced human preferences in agent behavior.
items.zh: 实现了一种新的无梯度上下文内学习方法，用于基于人类反馈的强化学习（RLHF），可以根据各种形式的人类输入生成性能更高且与人类对齐的奖励函数。我们证明我们的方法可以轻松受益于并改进现有的人类奖励函数。同样，我们展示了我们的方法能够使用人类文本反馈来共同设计奖励函数，捕捉智能体行为中细微的人类偏好。

items.en: Unlike prior work L2R on using LLMs to aid reward design (Yu et al., 2023), \ourmethodis completely free of task-specific prompts, reward templates, as well as few-shot examples. In our experiments, \ourmethodsignificantly outperforms L2R due to its ability to generate and refine free-form, expressive reward programs. \ourmethod’s generality is made possible through three key algorithmic design choices: environment as context, evolutionary search, and reward reflection. First, by taking the environment source code as context, \ourmethodcan zero-shot generate executable reward functions from the backbone coding LLM (GPT-4). Then, \ourmethodsubstantially improves the quality of its rewards by performing evolutionary search, iteratively proposing batches of reward candidates and refining the most promising ones within the LLM context window. This in-context improvement is made effective via reward reflection, a textual summary of the reward quality based on policy training statistics that enables automated and targeted reward editing; see Fig. 3 for an example of \ourmethodzero-shot reward as well as various improvements accumulated during its optimization. To ensure that \ourmethodcan scale up its reward search to maximum potential, \ourmethodevaluates intermediate rewards using GPU-accelerated distributed reinforcement learning on IsaacGym (Makoviychuk et al., 2021), which offers up to three orders of magnitude in policy learning speed, making \ourmethodan extensive algorithm that scales naturally with more compute. See Fig. 2 for an overview. We are committed to open-sourcing all prompts, environments, and generated reward functions to promote further research on LLM-based reward design.
items.zh: 与先前使用LLM辅助奖励设计的工作L2R（Yu等人，2023）不同，我们的方法完全不需要特定任务的提示、奖励模板以及少样本示例。在我们的实验中，我们的方法由于能够生成和优化自由形式的表达性奖励程序，显著优于L2R。我们的方法的通用性通过三个关键算法设计选择实现：环境作为上下文、进化搜索和奖励反思。首先，通过将环境源代码作为上下文，我们的方法可以从骨干编码LLM（GPT-4）零样本生成可执行的奖励函数。然后，我们的方法通过执行进化搜索，迭代地提出一批奖励候选并在LLM上下文窗口内优化最有希望的候选，从而显著提高奖励质量。这种上下文内改进通过奖励反思变得有效，奖励反思是基于策略训练统计数据的奖励质量文本摘要，可实现自动化和有针对性的奖励编辑；图3展示了我们的方法零样本奖励及其在优化过程中积累的各种改进的示例。为了确保我们的方法能够最大化其奖励搜索潜力，我们的方法使用IsaacGym（Makoviychuk等人，2021）上的GPU加速分布式强化学习评估中间奖励，这提供了高达三个数量级的策略学习速度，使我们的方法成为一种广泛的算法，随着更多算力自然扩展。参见图2的概述。我们致力于开源所有提示、环境和生成的奖励函数，以促进基于LLM的奖励设计的进一步研究。

items.en: The goal of reward design is to return a shaped reward function for a ground-truth reward function that may be difficult to optimize directly (e.g., sparse rewards); this ground-truth reward function may only be accessed via queries by the designer. We first introduce the formal definition from Singh et al. (2010), which we then adapt to the program synthesis setting, which we call reward generation.
items.zh: 奖励设计的目标是为可能难以直接优化的真实奖励函数（例如稀疏奖励）返回一个塑形奖励函数；设计者可能只能通过查询访问这个真实奖励函数。我们首先介绍Singh等人（2010）的形式化定义，然后将其适应于程序合成设置，我们称之为奖励生成。

items.en: (Reward Design Problem (Singh et al., 2010)) A reward design problem (RDP) is a tuple P=⟨M,ℛ,πM,F⟩𝑃𝑀ℛsubscript𝜋𝑀𝐹P=\langle M,\mathcal{R},\pi_{M},F\rangle, where M=(S,A,T)𝑀𝑆𝐴𝑇M=(S,A,T) is the world model with state space S𝑆S, action space A𝐴A, and transition function T𝑇T. ℛℛ\mathcal{R} is the space of reward functions; 𝒜M​(⋅):ℛ→Π:subscript𝒜𝑀⋅→ℛΠ\mathcal{A}_{M}(\cdot):\mathcal{R}\rightarrow\Pi is a learning algorithm that outputs a policy π:S→Δ​(A):𝜋→𝑆Δ𝐴\pi:S\rightarrow\Delta(A) that optimizes reward R∈ℛ𝑅ℛR\in\mathcal{R} in the resulting Markov Decision Process (MDP), (M,R)𝑀𝑅(M,R); F:Π→ℝ:𝐹→ΠℝF:\Pi\rightarrow\mathbb{R} is the fitness function that produces a scalar evaluation of any policy, which may only be accessed via policy queries (i.e., evaluate the policy using the fitness function). In an RDP, the goal is to output a reward function R∈ℛ𝑅ℛR\in\mathcal{R} such that the policy π:=𝒜M​(R)assign𝜋subscript𝒜𝑀𝑅\pi:=\mathcal{A}_{M}(R) that optimizes R𝑅R achieves the highest fitness score F​(π)𝐹𝜋F(\pi).
items.zh: （奖励设计问题（Singh等人，2010））奖励设计问题（RDP）是一个元组P=⟨M,ℛ,πM,F⟩，其中M=(S,A,T)是世界模型，具有状态空间S、动作空间A和转移函数T。ℛ是奖励函数空间；𝒜M(·):ℛ→Π是一个学习算法，输出策略π:S→Δ(A)，该策略优化结果马尔可夫决策过程（MDP）(M,R)中的奖励R∈ℛ；F:Π→ℝ是适应度函数，对任何策略产生标量评估，只能通过策略查询访问（即使用适应度函数评估策略）。在RDP中，目标是输出一个奖励函数R∈ℛ，使得优化R的策略π:=𝒜M(R)获得最高的适应度分数F(π)。

items.en: Reward Generation Problem. In our problem setting, every component within a RDP is specified via code. Then, given a string l𝑙l that specifies the task, the objective of the reward generation problem is to output a reward function code R𝑅R such that F​(𝒜M​(R))𝐹subscript𝒜𝑀𝑅F(\mathcal{A}_{M}(R)) is maximized.
items.zh: 奖励生成问题。在我们的问题设置中，RDP中的每个组件都通过代码指定。然后，给定指定任务的字符串l，奖励生成问题的目标是输出一个奖励函数代码R，使得F(𝒜M(R))最大化。

items.en: consists of three algorithmic components: 1) environment as context that enables zero-shot generation of executable rewards, 2) evolutionary search that iteratively proposes and refines reward candidates, and 3) reward reflection that enables fine-grained reward improvement. See Alg. 1 for pseudocode; all prompts are included in App. A.
items.zh: 由三个算法组件组成：1）环境作为上下文，实现零样本生成可执行奖励；2）进化搜索，迭代提出和优化奖励候选；3）奖励反思，实现细粒度奖励改进。参见算法1的伪代码；所有提示包含在附录A中。

items.en: Reward design requires the environment specification to be provided to the LLM. We propose directly feeding the raw environment code (without the reward code, if exists) as context. That is, the LLM will quite literally take M𝑀M as context. This is intuitive for two reasons: First, coding LLMs are trained on native code written in existing programming languages, so we should expect their code generation capability to be stronger when we directly allow them to compose in the style and syntax they are trained on. Second, and more fundamentally, the environment source code typically reveals what the environment semantically entails and which variables can and should be used to compose a reward function for the specified task. Leveraging these insights, \ourmethodinstructs the coding LLM to directly return executable Python code with only generic reward design and formatting tips, such as exposing individual components in the reward as a dictionary output (for reasons that will be apparent in Sec. 3.3). This procedure is maximally scalable as the environment source code, by construction, must exist. see App. D for details.
items.zh: 奖励设计需要将环境规范提供给LLM。我们建议直接将原始环境代码（如果存在奖励代码，则不包括）作为上下文。也就是说，LLM将字面意义上将M作为上下文。这有两个直观原因：首先，编码LLM在现有编程语言的原生代码上训练，因此当我们直接允许它们以训练过的风格和语法进行编写时，它们的代码生成能力应该更强。其次，更根本的是，环境源代码通常揭示环境在语义上包含什么，以及哪些变量可以且应该用于为指定任务组成奖励函数。利用这些见解，我们的方法指示编码LLM直接返回可执行的Python代码，仅附带通用的奖励设计和格式化提示，例如将奖励中的各个组件作为字典输出暴露（原因将在第3.3节中明确）。这个过程最大程度地可扩展，因为环境源代码必然存在。详见附录D。

items.en: Remarkably, with only these minimal instructions, \ourmethodcan already zero-shot generate plausibly-looking rewards in diverse environments in its first attempts. An example \ourmethodoutput is shown in Fig. 3. As seen, \ourmethodadeptly composes over existing observation variables (e.g., fingertip_pos) in the provided environment code and produces a competent reward code – all without any environment-specific prompt engineering or reward templating. On the first try, however, the generated reward may not always be executable, and even if it is, it can be quite sub-optimal with respect to the task fitness metric F𝐹F. While we can improve the prompt with task-specific formatting and reward design hints, doing so does not scale to new tasks and hinders the overall generality of our system. How can we effectively overcome the sub-optimality of single-sample reward generation?
items.zh: 值得注意的是，仅凭这些最小指令，我们的方法已经可以在首次尝试中零样本生成各种环境中看似合理的奖励。图3显示了一个示例输出。如图所示，我们的方法熟练地组合了提供的环境代码中的现有观测变量（例如fingertip_pos），并生成了胜任的奖励代码——所有这些都没有任何特定于环境的提示工程或奖励模板。然而，在第一次尝试时，生成的奖励可能并不总是可执行，即使可执行，也可能相对于任务适应度度量F相当次优。虽然我们可以通过特定于任务的格式化和奖励设计提示来改进提示，但这无法扩展到新任务，并阻碍了我们系统的整体通用性。我们如何有效克服单样本奖励生成的次优性？

items.capEn: Figure 3: \ourmethodcan zero-shot generate executable rewards and then flexibly improve them with many distinct types of free-form modification, such as (1) changing the hyperparameter of existing reward components, (2) changing the functional form of existing reward components, and (3) introducing new reward components.
items.capZh: 图3：我们的方法可以零样本生成可执行奖励，然后灵活地通过多种不同类型的自由形式修改进行改进，例如（1）更改现有奖励组件的超参数，（2）更改现有奖励组件的函数形式，以及（3）引入新的奖励组件。

items.en: In this section, we will demonstrate how evolutionary search presents a natural solution that addresses the aforementioned execution error and sub-optimality challenges. In each iteration, \ourmethodsamples several independent outputs from the LLM (Line 5 in Alg. 1). Since the generations are i.i.d, the probability that all reward functions from an iteration are buggy exponentially decreases as the number of samples increases. We find that for all environments we consider, even sampling just a handful (16) of outputs contains at least one executable reward code in the first iteration.
items.zh: 在本节中，我们将展示进化搜索如何提供一种自然解决方案，解决上述执行错误和次优性挑战。在每次迭代中，我们的方法从LLM中采样几个独立输出（算法1第5行）。由于生成是独立同分布的，一次迭代中所有奖励函数都有错误的概率随着样本数量的增加而指数级下降。我们发现，对于所有考虑的环境，即使只采样少量（16个）输出，在第一次迭代中也至少包含一个可执行的奖励代码。

items.en: Giving executable reward functions from an earlier iteration, \ourmethodperforms in-context reward mutation, proposing a new improved reward function from an existing one based on textual feedback. Given the instruction-following and in-context improvement capabilities of LLMs, \ourmethodachieves this by simply specifying the mutation operator as a text prompt that suggests a few general ways to modify an existing reward code based on a textual summary of policy training (Sec. 3.3). Several illustrative reward modifications are visualized in Fig. 3. Through mutation, a new \ourmethoditeration will take the best-performing reward from the previous iteration as context and generate K𝐾K more i.i.d reward outputs from the LLM. This iterative optimization continues until a specified number of iterations has been reached. Finally, we perform multiple random restarts to find better global solution; this is a standard strategy in global optimization to overcome bad initial guesses. In all our experiments, \ourmethodconducts 5 independent runs per environment, and for each run, searches for 555 iterations with K=16𝐾16K=16 samples per iteration.
items.zh: 给定早期迭代中的可执行奖励函数，我们的方法执行上下文内奖励变异，基于文本反馈从现有奖励中提出新的改进奖励函数。利用LLM的指令遵循和上下文内改进能力，我们的方法通过简单地将变异算子指定为文本提示来实现，该提示基于策略训练的文本摘要（第3.3节）建议了几种修改现有奖励代码的通用方法。图3中可视化了几种说明性的奖励修改。通过变异，新的我们的方法迭代将前一次迭代中表现最好的奖励作为上下文，并从LLM生成K个更多独立同分布的奖励输出。这种迭代优化持续到达到指定的迭代次数。最后，我们执行多次随机重启以找到更好的全局解；这是全局优化中克服不良初始猜测的标准策略。在所有实验中，我们的方法每个环境进行5次独立运行，每次运行搜索5次迭代，每次迭代K=16个样本。

items.en: In order to ground the in-context reward mutation, we must be able to put into words the quality of the generated rewards. As we can query the task fitness function F𝐹F on the resulting policies, a simple strategy is to just provide this numerical score as the reward evaluation. While serving as the holistic ground-truth metric, the task fitness function itself lacks in credit assignment, providing no useful information on why a reward function works or not. To provide a more intricate and targeted diagnosis for the rewards, we propose to construct automated feedback that summarizes the policy training dynamics in texts. Specifically, given that \ourmethodreward functions are asked to expose their individual components in the reward program (e.g., reward_components in Fig. 3), we track the scalar values of all reward components at intermediate policy checkpoints throughout training. For instance, consider the illustrative example in Fig. 2, where the snapshot values of av_penalty are provided as a list in the reward feedback.
items.zh: 为了给上下文中的奖励变异提供依据，我们必须能够用语言描述生成奖励的质量。由于我们可以查询结果策略上的任务适应度函数 F，一个简单的策略就是直接提供这个数值分数作为奖励评估。虽然作为整体的真实指标，任务适应度函数本身缺乏信用分配，无法提供关于奖励函数为何有效或无效的有用信息。为了对奖励提供更细致和有针对性的诊断，我们建议构建自动反馈，以文本形式总结策略训练动态。具体来说，鉴于我们的方法要求奖励函数在奖励程序中暴露其各个组件（例如，图3中的 reward_components），我们在训练过程中跟踪所有奖励组件在中间策略检查点处的标量值。例如，考虑图2中的示例，其中 av_penalty 的快照值以列表形式提供在奖励反馈中。

items.en: This reward reflection procedure, though simple to construct, is important due to the algorithm-dependent nature of reward optimization (Booth et al., 2023). That is, whether a reward function is effective is influenced by the particular choice of RL algorithm, and the same reward may perform very differently even under the same optimizer given hyperparameter differences (Henderson et al., 2018; Agarwal et al., 2021). By providing detailed accounts on how well the RL algorithm optimizes individual reward components, reward reflection enables \ourmethodto produce more targeted reward editing and synthesize reward functions that better synergize with the fixed RL algorithm.
items.zh: 这种奖励反思过程虽然构建简单，但由于奖励优化的算法依赖性，它非常重要（Booth 等人，2023）。也就是说，奖励函数是否有效受到特定 RL 算法选择的影响，即使在相同的优化器下，由于超参数差异，相同的奖励也可能表现非常不同（Henderson 等人，2018；Agarwal 等人，2021）。通过提供关于 RL 算法如何优化各个奖励组件的详细说明，奖励反思使我们的方法能够产生更有针对性的奖励编辑，并合成与固定 RL 算法更好协同的奖励函数。

items.en: We thoroughly evaluate \ourmethodon a diverse suite of robot embodiments and tasks, testing its ability to generate reward functions, solve new tasks, and incorporate various forms of human input. We use GPT-4 (OpenAI, 2023), in particular the gpt-4-0314 variant, as the backbone LLM for all LLM-based reward-design algorithms unless specified otherwise. Qualitative videos, reward examples, and open-source code are on our project website: https://eureka-research.github.io.
items.zh: 我们在多种机器人形态和任务上全面评估了我们的方法，测试其生成奖励函数、解决新任务以及整合各种形式的人类输入的能力。我们使用 GPT-4（OpenAI，2023），特别是 gpt-4-0314 变体，作为所有基于 LLM 的奖励设计算法的骨干 LLM，除非另有说明。定性视频、奖励示例和开源代码可在我们的项目网站上找到：https://eureka-research.github.io。

items.en: Environments. Our environments consist of 10 distinct robots and 29 tasks implemented using the Isaac Gym simulator (Makoviychuk et al., 2021). First, we include 9 original environments from Isaac Gym (Isaac), covering a diverse set of robot morphologies from quadruped, bipedal, quadrotor, cobot arm, to dexterous hands. In addition to coverage over robot form factors, we ensure depth in our evaluation by including all 20 tasks from the Bidexterous Manipulation (Dexterity) benchmark (Chen et al., 2022). Dexterity contains 20 complex bi-manual tasks that require a pair of Shadow Hand to solve a wide range of complex manipulation skills, ranging from object handover to rotating a cup by 180 degrees. For the task description input to \ourmethod, we use the official description provided in the environment repository when possible. See App. B for details on all environments. It is worth noting that both benchmarks are publicly released concurrently, or after the GPT-4 knowledge cut-off date (September 2021), so GPT-4 is unlikely to have accumulated extensive internet knowledge about these tasks, making them ideal testbeds for assessing \ourmethod’s reward generation capability compared to measurable human-engineered reward functions.
items.zh: 环境。我们的环境包括 10 种不同的机器人和 29 个任务，使用 Isaac Gym 模拟器实现（Makoviychuk 等人，2021）。首先，我们包含来自 Isaac Gym（Isaac）的 9 个原始环境，涵盖了从四足、双足、四旋翼、协作臂到灵巧手的多种机器人形态。除了机器人形态的覆盖范围，我们还通过包含来自 Bidexterous Manipulation（Dexterity）基准测试的所有 20 个任务来确保评估的深度（Chen 等人，2022）。Dexterity 包含 20 个复杂的双手任务，需要一对 Shadow Hand 解决各种复杂的操作技能，从物体交接将杯子旋转 180 度。对于输入到我们方法的任务描述，我们尽可能使用环境仓库中提供的官方描述。所有环境的详细信息见附录 B。值得注意的是，这两个基准测试都是公开发布的，或者是在 GPT-4 知识截止日期（2021 年 9 月）之后发布的，因此 GPT-4 不太可能积累关于这些任务的广泛互联网知识，这使得它们成为评估我们方法奖励生成能力（与可测量的人工设计的奖励函数相比）的理想测试平台。

items.capEn: Figure 4: \ourmethodoutperforms Human and L2R across all tasks. In particular, \ourmethodrealizes much greater gains on high-dimensional dexterity environments.
items.capZh: 图 4：我们的方法在所有任务上优于人类和 L2R。特别是，我们的方法在高维灵巧环境上实现了更大的增益。

items.en: L2R (Yu et al., 2023) proposes a two-stage LLM-prompting solution to generate templated rewards. For an environment and task specified in natural language, a first LLM is asked to fill in a natural language template describing the agent’s motion; then, a second LLM is asked to convert this “motion description” into code that calls a manually defined set of reward API primitives to write a reward program that sets their parameters. To make L2R competitive for our tasks, we define the motion description template to mimic the original L2R templates, and we construct the API reward primitives using the individual components of the original human rewards when possible. Note that this gives L2R an advantage as it has access to the original reward functions. Consistent with \ourmethod, we conduct 5 independent L2R runs per environment, and for each run, we generate 16 reward samples. See App. C for more details.
items.zh: L2R（Yu 等人，2023）提出了一种两阶段 LLM 提示解决方案来生成模板化奖励。对于以自然语言指定的环境和任务，首先要求一个 LLM 填写描述智能体运动的自然语言模板；然后，要求第二个 LLM 将此“运动描述”转换为代码，调用手动定义的奖励 API 原语集来编写奖励程序并设置其参数。为了使 L2R 在我们的任务中具有竞争力，我们定义了运动描述模板以模仿原始的 L2R 模板，并尽可能使用原始人类奖励的各个组件构建 API 奖励原语。请注意，这给了 L2R 一个优势，因为它可以访问原始奖励函数。与我们的方法一致，我们每个环境进行 5 次独立的 L2R 运行，每次运行生成 16 个奖励样本。更多细节见附录 C。

items.en: Human. These are the original shaped reward functions provided in our benchmark tasks. As these reward functions are written by active reinforcement learning researchers who designed the tasks, these reward functions represent the outcomes of expert-level human reward engineering.
items.zh: 人类。这些是我们的基准任务中提供的原始成形奖励函数。由于这些奖励函数是由设计任务的活动强化学习研究人员编写的，因此这些奖励函数代表了专家级人类奖励工程的结果。

items.en: Sparse. These are identical to the fitness functions F𝐹F that we use to evaluate the quality of the generated rewards. Like Human, these are also provided by the benchmark. On Dexterity tasks, they are uniformly binary indicator functions that measure task success; on Isaac tasks, they vary in functional forms depending on the nature of the task. See App. B for a description of the ground-truth scoring metric for all tasks.
items.zh: 稀疏。这些与用于评估生成奖励质量的适应度函数 F 相同。与人类一样，这些也由基准测试提供。在 Dexterity 任务上，它们统一是衡量任务成功的二元指示函数；在 Isaac 任务上，它们根据任务性质具有不同的函数形式。所有任务的真实评分指标描述见附录 B。

items.en: Policy Learning. For each task, all final reward functions are optimized using the same RL algorithm with the same set of hyperparameters. Isaac and Dexterity share a well-tuned PPO implementation (Schulman et al., 2017; Makoviichuk & Makoviychuk, 2021), and we use this implementation and the task-specific PPO hyperparameters without any modification. Note that these task hyperparameters are tuned to make the official human-engineered rewards work well. For each reward, we run 5 independent PPO training runs and report the average of the maximum task metric values achieved by policy checkpoints as the reward’s performance.
items.zh: 策略学习。对于每个任务，所有最终奖励函数都使用相同的 RL 算法和相同的超参数集进行优化。Isaac 和 Dexterity 共享一个经过良好调整的 PPO 实现（Schulman 等人，2017；Makoviichuk & Makoviychuk，2021），我们使用此实现和任务特定的 PPO 超参数，不做任何修改。请注意，这些任务超参数经过调整以使官方人工设计的奖励函数工作良好。对于每个奖励，我们运行 5 次独立的 PPO 训练，并报告策略检查点达到的最大任务指标值的平均值作为奖励的性能。

items.en: Reward Evaluation Metrics. For Isaac tasks, since the task metric F𝐹F for each task varies in semantic meaning and scale, we report the human normalized score for \ourmethodand L2R, Method−Sparse|Human−Sparse|MethodSparseHumanSparse\frac{\texttt{Method}-\texttt{Sparse}}{\lvert\texttt{Human}-\texttt{Sparse}\rvert}. This metric provides a holistic measure of how \ourmethodrewards fare against human-expert rewards with respect to the ground-truth task metric. For Dexterity, since all tasks are evaluated using the binary success function, we directly report success rates.
items.zh: 奖励评估指标。对于 Isaac 任务，由于每个任务的任务指标 F 在语义和尺度上各不相同，我们报告我们的方法和 L2R 的人类归一化分数：Method−Sparse|Human−Sparse|。该指标提供了我们的方法奖励相对于人类专家奖励在真实任务指标上的整体衡量。对于 Dexterity，由于所有任务都使用二元成功函数进行评估，我们直接报告成功率。

items.en: outperforms human rewards. In Figure 4, we report the aggregate results on the two benchmarks. Notably, \ourmethodexceeds or performs on par to human level on all Isaac tasks and 15 out of 20 tasks on Dexterity (see App. E for a per-task breakdown). In contrast, L2R, while comparable on low-dimensional tasks (e.g., CartPole, BallBalance), lags significantly behind on high-dimensional tasks. Despite being provided access to some of the same reward components as Human, L2R still underperforms \ourmethodafter its initial iteration, when both methods have had the same number of reward queries. As expected, L2R’s lack of expressivity severely limits its performance. In contrast, \ourmethodgenerates free-form rewards from scratch without any domain-specific knowledge and performs substantially better. In App. E, we ablate GPT-4 with GPT-3.5 and find \ourmethoddegrades in performance but still matches or exceeds human-level on most Isaac tasks, indicating that its general principles can be readily applied to coding LLMs of varying qualities.
items.zh: 优于人类奖励。在图 4 中，我们报告了两个基准测试的汇总结果。值得注意的是，我们的方法在所有 Isaac 任务和 Dexterity 的 20 个任务中的 15 个上达到或超过人类水平（每个任务的细分见附录 E）。相比之下，L2R 虽然在低维任务（例如 CartPole、BallBalance）上具有可比性，但在高维任务上明显落后。尽管 L2R 可以访问与人类相同的一些奖励组件，但在初始迭代后，当两种方法具有相同数量的奖励查询时，L2R 仍然不如我们的方法。正如预期的那样，L2R 缺乏表现力严重限制了其性能。相比之下，我们的方法从头开始生成自由形式的奖励，没有任何领域特定知识，并且表现明显更好。在附录 E 中，我们将 GPT-4 与 GPT-3.5 进行消融，发现我们的方法性能下降，但在大多数 Isaac 任务上仍达到或超过人类水平，这表明其一般原则可以轻松应用于不同质量的编码 LLM。

items.capEn: Figure 5: \ourmethodprogressively produces better rewards via in-context evolutionary reward search.
items.capZh: 图 5：我们的方法通过上下文进化奖励搜索逐步产生更好的奖励。

items.en: consistently improves over time. In Fig. 5, we visualize the average performance of the cumulative best \ourmethodrewards after each evolution iteration. Moreover, we study an ablation, \ourmethodw.o. Evolution (32 Samples), which performs only the initial reward generation step, sampling the same number of reward functions as two iterations in the original \ourmethod. This ablation helps study, given a fixed number of reward function budget, whether it is more advantageous to perform the \ourmethodevolution or simply sample more first-attempt rewards without iterative improvement. As seen, on both benchmarks, \ourmethodrewards steadily improve and eventually surpass human rewards in performance despite sub-par initial performances. This consistent improvement also cannot be replaced by just sampling more in the first iteration as the ablation’s performances are lower than \ourmethodafter 2 iterations on both benchmarks. Together, these results demonstrate that \ourmethod’s novel evolutionary optimization is indispensable for its final performance.
items.zh: 随时间持续改进。在图 5 中，我们可视化了每次进化迭代后累积最佳我们的方法奖励的平均性能。此外，我们研究了一个消融实验，即我们的方法 w.o. Evolution（32 个样本），它只执行初始奖励生成步骤，采样与原始我们的方法两次迭代相同数量的奖励函数。这个消融实验有助于研究，在给定固定数量的奖励函数预算下，是执行我们的方法进化更有利，还是简单地采样更多首次尝试的奖励而不进行迭代改进。如图所示，在两个基准测试上，我们的方法奖励稳步提高，并最终在性能上超过人类奖励，尽管初始性能不佳。这种持续改进也不能仅仅通过第一次迭代中采样更多样本来替代，因为消融实验在两个基准测试上的性能都低于经过 2 次迭代后的我们的方法。这些结果共同表明，我们的方法新颖的进化优化对其最终性能至关重要。

items.capZh: 图 6：Eureka 生成新颖的奖励。

items.en: generates novel rewards. We assess the novelty of \ourmethodrewards by computing the correlations between \ourmethodand human rewards on all Isaac tasks; see App. B for details on this procedure. Then, we plot the correlations against the human normalized scores on a scatter-plot in Figure 6, where each point represents a single \ourmethodreward on a single task. As shown, \ourmethodmostly generates weakly correlated reward functions that outperform the human ones. In addition, by examining the average correlation by task (App. E), we observe that the harder the task is, the less correlated the \ourmethodrewards. We hypothesize that human rewards are less likely to be near optimal for difficult tasks, leaving more room for \ourmethodrewards to be different and better. In a few cases, \ourmethodrewards are even negatively correlated with human rewards but perform significantly better, demonstrating that \ourmethodcan discover novel reward design principles that may run counter to human intuition; we illustrate these \ourmethodrewards in App. F.2.
items.zh: 生成新颖的奖励。我们通过计算我们的方法与人类奖励在所有 Isaac 任务上的相关性来评估我们的方法奖励的新颖性；此过程的详细信息见附录 B。然后，我们在图 6 的散点图中绘制相关性相对于人类归一化分数的关系，其中每个点代表单个任务上的单个我们的方法奖励。如图所示，我们的方法主要生成弱相关的奖励函数，这些函数优于人类奖励。此外，通过检查每个任务的平均相关性（附录 E），我们观察到任务越难，我们的方法奖励的相关性越低。我们假设对于困难任务，人类奖励不太可能接近最优，为我们的方法奖励留出了更多空间来变得不同且更好。在少数情况下，我们的方法奖励甚至与人类奖励负相关，但表现明显更好，这表明我们的方法可以发现可能违背人类直觉的新颖奖励设计原则；我们在附录 F.2 中展示了这些我们的方法奖励。

items.en: Reward reflection enables targeted improvement. To assess the importance of constructing reward reflection in the reward feedback, we evaluate an ablation, \ourmethod(No Reward Reflection), which reduces the reward feedback prompt to include only snapshot values of the task metric F𝐹F. Averaged over all Isaac tasks, \ourmethodwithout reward reflection reduces the average normalized score by 28.6%; in App. E, we provide detailed per-task breakdown and observe much greater performance deterioration on higher dimensional tasks. To provide qualitative analysis, in App. F.1, we include several examples in which \ourmethodutilizes the reward reflection to perform targeted reward editing.
items.zh: 奖励反思实现有针对性的改进。为了评估在奖励反馈中构建奖励反思的重要性，我们评估了一个消融实验，即我们的方法（无奖励反思），它将奖励反馈提示简化为仅包含任务指标 F 的快照值。在所有 Isaac 任务上平均，没有奖励反思的我们的方法将平均归一化分数降低了 28.6%；在附录 E 中，我们提供了每个任务的详细细分，并观察到在高维任务上性能下降更大。为了提供定性分析，在附录 F.1 中，我们包含了一些示例，其中我们的方法利用奖励反思进行有针对性的奖励编辑。

items.capEn: Figure 7: \ourmethodcan be flexibly combined with curriculum learning to acquire complex dexterous skills.
items.capZh: 图 7：我们的方法可以灵活地与课程学习结合，以获取复杂的灵巧技能。

items.en: with curriculum learning enables dexterous pen spinning. Finally, we investigate whether \ourmethodcan be used to solve a truly novel and challenging dexterous task. To this end, we propose pen spinning as a test bed. This task is highly dynamic and requires a Shadow Hand to continuously rotate a pen to achieve some pre-defined spinning patterns for as many cycles as possible. We consider a curriculum learning (Bengio et al., 2009) approach to break down the task into manageable components that can be independently solved by \ourmethod; similar approaches have been found successful for other coding LLM applications to decision making (Wang et al., 2023a). Specifically, we first instruct \ourmethodto generate a reward for re-orienting the pen to random target configurations. Then, using this pre-trained policy (Pre-Trained), we fine-tune it using the \ourmethodreward to reach the sequence of pen-spinning configurations (Fine-Tuned). To demonstrate the importance of curriculum learning, we also train a baseline policy from scratch using \ourmethodreward without the first-stage pre-training (Scratch). The RL training curves are shown in Figure 7. Eureka fine-tuning quickly adapts the policy to successfully spin the pen for many consecutive cycles along a specified spinning axis. In contrast, neither pre-trained or learning-from-scratch policies can complete even a single cycle of pen spinning. In addition, using this \ourmethodfine-tuning approach, we have also trained pen spinning policies for a variety of different spinning configurations; all pen spinning videos can be viewed on our project website, and experimental details are in App. D.1. These results demonstrate \ourmethod’s applicability to advanced policy learning approaches, which are often necessary for learning very complex skills.
items.zh: 与课程学习结合实现灵巧的笔旋转。最后，我们研究我们的方法是否可用于解决真正新颖且具有挑战性的灵巧任务。为此，我们提出笔旋转作为测试平台。该任务高度动态，需要 Shadow Hand 连续旋转笔以实现某些预定义的旋转模式，并尽可能多地旋转周期。我们考虑一种课程学习方法（Bengio 等人，2009），将任务分解为可由我们的方法独立解决的可管理组件；类似的方法已被发现对于其他编码 LLM 在决策制定中的应用是成功的（Wang 等人，2023a）。具体来说，我们首先指示我们的方法为将笔重新定向到随机目标配置生成奖励。然后，使用这个预训练的策略（Pre-Trained），我们使用我们的方法奖励对其进行微调，以达到笔旋转配置序列（Fine-Tuned）。为了证明课程学习的重要性，我们还从头开始训练一个基线策略，使用我们的方法奖励而没有第一阶段预训练（Scratch）。RL 训练曲线如图 7 所示。Eureka 微调快速调整策略，使其成功沿指定旋转轴连续旋转笔多个周期。相比之下，预训练和从头学习的策略都无法完成哪怕一个周期的笔旋转。此外，使用这种我们的方法微调方法，我们还为各种不同的旋转配置训练了笔旋转策略；所有笔旋转视频都可以在我们的项目网站上查看，实验细节在附录 D.1 中。这些结果证明了我们的方法适用于高级策略学习方法，这些方法对于学习非常复杂的技能通常是必要的。

items.en: In addition to automated reward design, \ourmethodenables a new gradient-free in-context learning approach to RL from Human Feedback (RLHF) that can readily incorporate various types of human inputs to generate more performant and human-aligned reward functions.
items.zh: 除了自动奖励设计，我们的方法还启用了一种新的无梯度上下文学习方法，用于基于人类反馈的强化学习（RLHF），该方法可以轻松整合各种类型的人类输入，以生成性能更好且与人类对齐的奖励函数。

items.capEn: Figure 8: \ourmethodeffectively improves and benefits from human reward initialization.
items.capZh: 图 8：我们的方法有效改进并受益于人类奖励初始化。

items.en: can improve and benefit from human reward functions. We study whether starting with a human reward function initialization, a common scenario in real-world RL applications, is advantageous for \ourmethod. Importantly, incorporating human initialization requires no modification to \ourmethod – we can simply substitute the raw human reward function as the output of the first \ourmethoditeration. To investigate this, we select several tasks from Dexterity that differ in the relative performances between the original \ourmethodand human rewards. The full results are shown in Figure 8. As shown, regardless of the quality of the human rewards, \ourmethodimproves and benefits from human rewards as \ourmethod(Human Init.) is uniformly better than both \ourmethodand Human on all tasks. This suggests that \ourmethod’s in-context reward improvement capability is largely independent of the quality of the base reward. Furthermore, the fact that \ourmethodcan significantly improve over human rewards even when they are highly sub-optimal hints towards an interesting hypothesis: human designers are generally knowledgeable about relevant state variables but are less proficient at designing rewards using them. This makes intuitive sense as identifying relevant state variables that should be included in the reward function involves mostly common sense reasoning, but reward design requires specialized knowledge and experience in RL. Together, these results demonstrate \ourmethod’s reward assistant capability, perfectly complementing human designers’ knowledge about useful state variables and making up for their less proficiency on how to design rewards using them. In App. F.3, we provide several examples of \ourmethod(Human Init.) steps.
items.zh: 可以改进并受益于人类奖励函数。我们研究了从人类奖励函数初始化开始（这是现实世界强化学习应用中的常见场景）是否对\ourmethod有利。重要的是，引入人类初始化不需要修改\ourmethod——我们只需将原始人类奖励函数替换为第一次\ourmethod迭代的输出即可。为了研究这一点，我们从Dexterity中选择了几个任务，这些任务在原始\ourmethod和人类奖励之间的相对性能有所不同。完整结果如图8所示。如图所示，无论人类奖励的质量如何，\ourmethod都能改进并受益于人类奖励，因为\ourmethod（人类初始化）在所有任务上均优于\ourmethod和人类奖励。这表明\ourmethod的上下文奖励改进能力在很大程度上独立于基础奖励的质量。此外，\ourmethod即使在人类奖励高度次优时也能显著改进，这暗示了一个有趣的假设：人类设计者通常了解相关的状态变量，但在使用这些变量设计奖励方面不太熟练。这直观上是合理的，因为识别应包含在奖励函数中的相关状态变量主要涉及常识推理，而奖励设计需要强化学习方面的专业知识和经验。这些结果共同展示了\ourmethod的奖励辅助能力，完美补充了人类设计者对有用状态变量的知识，并弥补了他们在如何使用这些变量设计奖励方面的不足。在附录F.3中，我们提供了\ourmethod（人类初始化）步骤的几个示例。

items.capEn: Figure 9: \ourmethodcan incorporate human feedback to modify rewards and induce more human-aligned policys.
items.capZh: 图9：\ourmethod可以整合人类反馈来修改奖励并诱导更符合人类行为的策略。

items.en: Reward reflection via human feedback induces aligned behavior. So far, all \ourmethodrewards are optimized against a fixed, black-box task fitness function F𝐹F. This task metric, however, may not fully align with human intent. Moreover, in many open-ended tasks, F𝐹F may not be available in the first place (Fan et al., 2022). In these challenging scenarios, we propose to augment \ourmethodby having humans step in and put into words the reward reflection in terms of the desired behavior and correction. We investigate this capability in \ourmethodby teaching a Humanoid agent how to run purely from textual reward reflection; in App. F.4, we show the exact sequence of human feedback and \ourmethodrewards. Then, we conduct a user study asking 20 unfamiliar users to indicate their preferences between two policy rollout videos shown in random order, one trained with human reward reflection (\ourmethod-HF) and the other one trained with the original best \ourmethodreward; the details are in App. D.3. As shown in Tab. 1, the \ourmethod-HF agent is preferred by a large majority of our users, successfully trading off speed in favor of stability. In Fig. 9, we illustrate the evolution of Eureka learned behaviors after each human feedback. Qualitative, we indeed see that the \ourmethod-HF agent progressively acquires safer and more stable gait, as instructed by the human. On our project website, we include the videos for each of the intermediate \ourmethod-HF policies as well as the their associated \ourmethodrewards.
items.zh: 通过人类反馈进行奖励反思可诱导对齐行为。到目前为止，所有\ourmethod奖励都是针对固定的黑盒任务适应度函数F进行优化的。然而，这个任务指标可能并不完全符合人类意图。此外，在许多开放式任务中，F可能根本不可用（Fan等人，2022）。在这些具有挑战性的场景中，我们建议通过让人类介入并以文字形式表达关于期望行为和纠正的奖励反思来增强\ourmethod。我们通过教授一个人形智能体纯粹从文本奖励反思中跑步来研究\ourmethod的这种能力；在附录F.4中，我们展示了人类反馈和\ourmethod奖励的确切序列。然后，我们进行了一项用户研究，要求20名不熟悉的用户以随机顺序观看两个策略回放视频，并指出他们的偏好，其中一个视频使用人类奖励反思（\ourmethod-HF）训练，另一个使用原始最佳\ourmethod奖励训练；详细信息见附录D.3。如表1所示，绝大多数用户更喜欢\ourmethod-HF智能体，它成功地在速度与稳定性之间进行了权衡。在图9中，我们展示了每次人类反馈后Eureka学习行为的演变。定性来看，我们确实看到\ourmethod-HF智能体按照人类的指示逐渐获得了更安全、更稳定的步态。在我们的项目网站上，我们包含了每个中间\ourmethod-HF策略的视频及其相关的\ourmethod奖励。

items.en: Reward Design. Reward engineering is a long-standing challenge in reinforcement learning (Singh et al., 2010; Sutton & Barto, 2018). The most common reward design method is manual trial-and-error (Knox et al., 2023; Booth et al., 2023). Inverse reinforcement learning (IRL) infers reward functions from demonstrations (Abbeel & Ng, 2004; Ziebart et al., 2008; Ho & Ermon, 2016), but it requires expensive expert data collection, which may not be available, and outputs non-interpretable black-box reward functions. Several prior works have studied automated reward search through evolutionary algorithms (Niekum et al., 2010; Chiang et al., 2019; Faust et al., 2019). These early attempts are limited to task-specific implementations of evolutionary algorithms that search over only parameters within provided reward templates. Recent works have also proposed using pretrained foundation models that can produce reward functions for new tasks (Ma et al., 2022; 2023; Fan et al., 2022; Du et al., 2023a; Karamcheti et al., 2023; Du et al., 2023b; Kwon et al., 2023). Most of these approaches output scalar rewards that lack interpretability and do not naturally admit the capability to improve or adapt rewards on-the-fly. In contrast, \ourmethodadeptly generates free-form, white-box reward code and effectively in-context improves.
items.zh: 奖励设计。奖励工程是强化学习中一个长期存在的挑战（Singh等人，2010；Sutton & Barto，2018）。最常见的奖励设计方法是手动试错（Knox等人，2023；Booth等人，2023）。逆强化学习（IRL）从演示中推断奖励函数（Abbeel & Ng，2004；Ziebart等人，2008；Ho & Ermon，2016），但它需要昂贵的专家数据收集，这可能不可用，并且输出不可解释的黑盒奖励函数。一些先前的工作研究了通过进化算法进行自动奖励搜索（Niekum等人，2010；Chiang等人，2019；Faust等人，2019）。这些早期尝试仅限于任务特定的进化算法实现，这些算法仅在提供的奖励模板内搜索参数。最近的工作还提出使用预训练的基础模型，这些模型可以为新任务生成奖励函数（Ma等人，2022；2023；Fan等人，2022；Du等人，2023a；Karamcheti等人，2023；Du等人，2023b；Kwon等人，2023）。这些方法大多输出标量奖励，缺乏可解释性，并且自然不具备即时改进或调整奖励的能力。相比之下，\ourmethod熟练地生成自由形式的白盒奖励代码，并有效地进行上下文改进。

items.en: Code Large Language Models for Decision Making. Recent works have considered using coding LLMs (Austin et al., 2021; Chen et al., 2021; Rozière et al., 2023) to generate grounded and structured programmatic output for decision making and robotics problems (Liang et al., 2023; Singh et al., 2023; Wang et al., 2023b; Huang et al., 2023; Wang et al., 2023a; Liu et al., 2023; Silver et al., 2023; Ding et al., 2023; Lin et al., 2023; Xie et al., 2023). However, most of these works rely on known motion primitives to carry out robot actions and do not apply to robot tasks that require low-level skill learning, such as dexterous manipulation. The closest to our work is a recent work (Yu et al., 2023) that also explores using LLMs to aid reward design. Their approach, however, requires domain-specific task descriptions and reward templates, which demand substantial domain knowledge and limit the expressivity of the generated reward functions.
items.zh: 用于决策的代码大语言模型。最近的工作考虑了使用编码LLM（Austin等人，2021；Chen等人，2021；Rozière等人，2023）为决策和机器人问题生成基于基础的结构化程序输出（Liang等人，2023；Singh等人，2023；Wang等人，2023b；Huang等人，2023；Wang等人，2023a；Liu等人，2023；Silver等人，2023；Ding等人，2023；Lin等人，2023；Xie等人，2023）。然而，这些工作大多依赖于已知的运动基元来执行机器人动作，并不适用于需要低级技能学习的机器人任务，例如灵巧操作。与我们的工作最接近的是最近的一项工作（Yu等人，2023），该工作也探索了使用LLM辅助奖励设计。然而，他们的方法需要特定领域的任务描述和奖励模板，这需要大量的领域知识，并限制了生成奖励函数的表达能力。

items.en: Evolution with LLMs. Implementing evolutionary algorithms with LLMs has been explored in recent works in the context of neural architecture search (Chen et al., 2023; Nasir et al., 2023), prompt engineering (Guo et al., 2023), as well as morphology design (Lehman et al., 2022). Ours is the first to apply this principle to reward design. Unlike prior approaches, \ourmethoddoes not need humans to provide the initial candidates or few-shot prompting. Furthermore, \ourmethodintroduces novel reward reflection mechanism that enables more targeted and effective reward mutation.
items.zh: 使用LLM进行进化。使用LLM实现进化算法已在最近的工作中探索，涉及神经架构搜索（Chen等人，2023；Nasir等人，2023）、提示工程（Guo等人，2023）以及形态设计（Lehman等人，2022）。我们是第一个将这一原理应用于奖励设计的工作。与先前的方法不同，\ourmethod不需要人类提供初始候选或少量示例提示。此外，\ourmethod引入了新颖的奖励反思机制，实现了更有针对性和更有效的奖励变异。

items.en: We have presented \ourmethod, a universal reward design algorithm powered by coding large language models and in-context evolutionary search. Without any task-specific prompt engineering or human intervention, \ourmethodachieves human-level reward generation on a wide range of robots and tasks. \ourmethod’s particular strength in learning dexterity solves dexterous pen spinning for the first time with a curriculum learning approach. Finally, \ourmethodenables a gradient-free approach to reinforcement learning from human feedback, readily incorporating human reward initialization and textual feedback to better steer its reward generation. The versatility and substantial performance gains of \ourmethodsuggest that the simple principle of combining large language models with evolutionary algorithms is a general and scalable approach to reward design, an insight that may be generally applicable to difficult, open-ended search problems.
items.zh: 我们提出了\ourmethod，一种由编码大语言模型和上下文进化搜索驱动的通用奖励设计算法。无需任何特定任务的提示工程或人工干预，\ourmethod在广泛的机器人和任务上实现了人类水平的奖励生成。\ourmethod在学习灵巧操作方面的特殊优势，首次通过课程学习方法解决了灵巧转笔问题。最后，\ourmethod实现了一种无梯度的基于人类反馈的强化学习方法，能够轻松整合人类奖励初始化和文本反馈，以更好地引导其奖励生成。\ourmethod的多功能性和显著的性能提升表明，将大语言模型与进化算法相结合的简单原则是一种通用且可扩展的奖励设计方法，这一见解可能普遍适用于困难的、开放式的搜索问题。
